project.title: LC-MS/MS analysis of Gd-IgA1 and secretary TfR1 cross-linked complex
project.projectDescription: The retention of galactose-deficient IgA1 (Gd-IgA1) in the glomerular mesangium is a hallmark of IgA nephropathy (IgAN). The immune complex formed by Gd-IgA1 binds to transferrin receptor 1 (TfR1) and deposits on mesangial cell, inducing cell proliferation, inflammatory response, matrix secretion, etc., causes organic damage and dysfunction of the kidney. Even though, the mechanism of how Gd-IgA1 recognize and bind to TfR1 is unclear.  In this experiment, we ami to determine the specific interaction region responsible for Gd-IgA1 and TfR1. We prepared a complex of Gd-IgA1 and TfR1 by incubating the two proteins under acidic conditions and stabilizing it using the chemical cross-linking agent glutaraldehyde. And then, the gel bands corresponding to the Gd-IgA1-TfR1 complex was exexcised from the SDS-PAGE gel and analyzed by high-resolution mass spectrometry. This analysis identified fragments containing the hinge peptide sequence, with the R276 residue interacting with the E350 and D356 residues in the apical domain of TfR1. Our results reveal that the hinge peptide of Gd-IgA1 interacts specifically with the apical domain of TfR1.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: purified TfR1 protein was mixed with Gd-IgA1 at a molar ratio of 1:3 in buffer A (pH 5.5) and incubated at room temperature for 2 hours. After that, the 1-Ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl) carbodiimide (EDC) and N-Hydroxysulfosuccinimide (sulfo-NHS) solutions (Cytiva, BR100050) were mixed with a ratio of (1:5, v: v) and transferred to the protein mixture at a ratio of (1:10, v: v) drop by drop. The sample continued to incubate under room temperature for another 2 hours, and then equal volume of 100mM tris buffer (pH 5.5) was used to end the crosslinking reaction. 100 μL samples (about 50-60μg proteins) were denatured by adding 20 μL 5 loading buffer and treated in 100 ℃ heating, and then separated by SDS-PAGE with a gradient gel (4-15%). The Gd-IgA1 and TfR1 crosslinked band in the gel was cut and analyzed by mass spectrometry.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Liquid Chromatography Tandem Mass Spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) on an Orbitrap Fusion Lumos instrument (Thermo Fisher Scientific) with a High-Energy Collisional Dissociation (HCD) fragmentation function was employed for LC-MS/MS analyses. The gel samples were firstly cut into little pieces (~1 mm3) and incubated in 50 mM NH4HCO3/acetonitrile solution (1:1, v: v) to destain. Then the gel was washed using 100% acetonitrile for 2 times and dehydrated in vacuum. The samples were treated with DTT and IAA to reductive alkylation. Protein digested using trypsin overnight, and peptides were recollected and concentrated using a vacuum concentrator. In LC-MS/MS analysis, digested products were separated by a 120 min gradient elution at a flow rate 0.300 µL/min with the Thermo Ultimate 3000 nano-UPLC system which was directly interfaced with the Thermo Fusion LUMOS mass spectrometer. The analytical column was an Acclaim PepMap RSLC column (75 µm ID, 250 mm length, C18). Mobile phase A consisted of 0.1% formic acid, and mobile phase B consisted of 100% acetonitrile and 0.1% formic acid. The Fusion LUMOS mass spectrometer was operated in the data-dependent acquisition mode using Xcalibur 4.1.50 software and there is a single full-scan mass spectrum in the Orbitrap (375-1500 m/z, 60,000 resolution) followed by data-dependent MS/MS scans. The MS/MS spectra from each LC-MS/MS run were searched against the selected database using the software Byonic.
